Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Eddie Stewart

RULING:
Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company's motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden that Shellie Stewart is incompetent as a witness. The court reaffirms the prior tentative ruling that the declarations of Randy George and Shellie Stewart create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (monolith asbestos gun cement) attributable to defendant. The objections to the declaration of Mr. George and Mr. Stewart are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to issues 2 and 3. Order signed in open court. RULING:
Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Defendant submitted to the Court's tentative ruling issued on 9/8/2010. The Court adopted its' tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding plaintiff's exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant.
